Home/International Removals/Qatar
The Move

Qatar is a route we know well, and one where timing your visa makes all the difference.

British professionals relocate to Doha and the surrounding areas regularly, often for long-term contracts. Customs clearance is closely tied to your residence permit, so we plan your shipping date around it from the outset.

Sea freight suits a full household, and air freight covers anything you would rather have on arrival.

Popular destinations we ship to
DohaThe PearlWest BayLusailAl WakrahAl Rayyan
Typical Transit Times
Sole-use container5–6 weeks
Shared (groupage) container6–9 weeks
Air freight1–2 weeks
Times shown are to port. Please allow additional time for Qatari customs clearance on arrival. We recommend booking 2 to 4 months ahead of your move date.
Your options

Choose the right way to ship

Every move to Qatar is different, so we shape the service around yours.

Sea · Sole-use

Your own 20ft or 40ft container

Your belongings travel alone, packed and sealed at your door and not opened again until they reach you. The fastest and most secure sea option.

Best for: full household moves
Sea · Shared

Groupage / part-load container

Your goods share container space with other shipments on the same route, splitting the cost between several moves. Slightly longer transit, but excellent value.

Best for: smaller moves, flexible budgets
Air Freight

Fast-track for essentials

Air freight suits the things you need soon after you land, such as documents, clothing and everyday items. It arrives in a fraction of the time.

Best for: essentials & smaller shipments
Excess Baggage

A few boxes or suitcases

Not moving a whole household? Our excess baggage service is a simple, affordable way to send personal items and smaller consignments overseas.

Best for: light movers & students
Good to know

Qatar customs is thorough, and a few rules are worth knowing early

Clearance in Qatar is generally tied to your Qatar ID and residence permit, so having these ready matters. A small number of items are tightly controlled, and we flag these before anything is packed.

Alcohol

Alcohol is strictly controlled and cannot travel in a household shipment.

Media & printed material

Books, film and magazines may be reviewed for content on arrival.

Residence permit tied timing

Clearance is generally linked to your Qatar ID and residence permit being in place.

New goods & electronics

New and high value items can attract duty, so keep your receipts.
